demographics.living_situation: lives at home; adult child present and helping

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My belly has completely blown up in just a few days, I've lost all the flesh on my arms, and there is a constant, deep throbbing ache under my right ribs. (Speaking with help from my adult child, who is here with me.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The swelling and the ache have got much worse over the past week, I can't eat and I feel wrung out, and my water tablets don't seem to be doing anything anymore.

symptom_narrative.onset: a dull ache under the right ribs came on gradually about 2 months ago; over the last week the belly swelling and the ache have got much worse, quite suddenly
symptom_narrative.duration: about 2 months of dull ache, dramatically worse over the last 7 days
symptom_narrative.character: a constant, deep throbbing ache under the right ribs; the belly feels massively full and tight
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the ache is severe and never lets up; I feel completely wiped out and weak
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: under the right ribs and across a hugely swollen, tight belly
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, present all the time and getting worse, not coming and going
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing seems to help; sitting propped up eases the breathing a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the swelling keeps building no matter what; lying flat makes it harder to breathe
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no appetite at all, feeling full after a mouthful, the whites of my eyes and my skin look yellow, and I feel breathless when the belly is tight
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had some fluid and swelling before from my liver, but never anything like this, and never this fast
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm basically stuck in bed now, too weak and uncomfortable to get up

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: cirrhosis (scarring of the liver) from a hepatitis C infection I've had for years
past_medical_history.how_managed: followed up for my liver; been on water tablets for the fluid

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing fluid build-up in the belly from my liver, usually mild until now

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home with family support; adult child is present and made the call because of how quickly things have worsened

objective_data_offered.value: the whites of the eyes and the skin look yellow (jaundiced)
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: colour seen on a phone video under home lighting; not a clinician assessment

objective_data_offered.value: the belly is very large, tight and shiny-looking, much bigger than usual
he has lost a lot of weight fast — about 10 kg — and his arms and face look wasted and thin
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family estimate, not weighed on a device during the call

objective_data_offered.value: he is bedbound and too weak to get up, hasn't been able to eat or drink, and the untouched supplement drinks are still by the bed
he looks short of breath and more comfortable propped upright than lying flat because the swollen belly seems to push up on his chest
